item.title: Creepy doesn't have a sexual orientation
item.description: Gay regular at the bar I bounce at was getting creepy unwanted advances from another guy in the bar. I walked over and obviously asked if he felt safe and if he wanted me to put a stop to it and possibly kick the guy out. He seemed baffled when I asked and said it was fine but came over a few minutes later and asked if I still could so I did. I do this for women all the time but he said he never saw a bouncer check on a guy before in that situation. Harassment is Harassment male or otherwise and it made me more than a little sadto hear that.
item.dc:creator: Kaizer5243

item.dc:creator: Mordy9

item.title: Nursing a Moose back to health
item.description:
Meet Moose. Last year he wandered up to our neighborhood covered in injuries, underweight, and worn out
We took him to the vet where we found out he had hookworms, fleas, ear mites, and heartworms. The poor guy was harboring entire ecosystems of parasites
The vet prescribed dewormer, flea meds, ear drops, Heartworm preventative, vaccines, and weeks of antibiotics to weaken the existing heartworms before he received the heartworm treatment shots.
Slowly his face healed and his fur grew back, the flea meds, ear drops, and dewormer did their jobs but he still had a tough road ahead with the heartworm treatment.
After he finished the antibiotics it was time for the heartworm shots. Three shots given on a set schedule with strict crate rest. No zoomies, no jumping, no excitement of any kind or a dead heartworm piece could cause a clot. The prospect of a potential “wormbolism” made the entire process pretty scary for me but he made it through the shots and the weeks of rest that followed.
It’s been almost a year since we started this process and he just went in for his 6 month follow up, the tests revealed he is now heartworm negative. TLDR: rescue dog Moose had injuries and all the parasites, now he’s healthy and likes to sleep on the couch with his feet in the air
item.dc:creator: ihatepickingusernames8

item.title: Aerospace Engineer Judith Love Cohen & son
item.description: Judith Love Cohen, who helped create the Abort-Guidance System which rescued the Apollo 13 astronauts, went to work on the day she was in labor. She took a printout of a problem she was working on to the hospital. She called her boss and said she finished the problem and gave birth to Jack Black https://www.reddit.com/r/todayilearned/comments/mxsdzu/til_judith_love_cohen_who_helped_create_the/ FP edit: Thanks for the accolades!
item.dc:creator: IliveIdyeIliveagain

item.title: World's Largest Four-Day Work Week Experiment Begins
item.description: https://gizmodo.com/four-day-work-week-wfh-remote-work-return-to-office-1849022441 Companies in the United Kingdom will see how a four-day work week affects their productivity and employee happiness. It’s part of a six-month-long pilot program, the largest of its kind. This means thousands of workers in the UK will be able to savor an extra day off, with no reduction in pay.
